responsibilities: Verification of code quality (lint, build, test) across Node.js 20 and 22
Release gate execution to check release readiness
Smoke testing of the packaged build for npm publish
outOfScope: Actual deployment or publishing to npm

English.purpose: This document defines the automated continuous integration workflow for the ArchSpine project. It ensures every push to main and every pull request undergoes a standardized set of checks: linting, building, testing, protocol validation, documentation building, and a release gate. The workflow also includes a package smoke test to verify that the npm package can be built and is publishable.
English.context_and_audience: The intended audience is developers and maintainers of the ArchSpine project who need to understand how changes are verified before merging and before release. It also serves as a single source of truth for CI behavior, helping contributors and release managers ensure consistency and quality.
key_takeaways: The workflow runs on pushes to main and pull requests, plus manual triggers.
There are four main jobs: verify (Node 20 & 22), release gate, docs build, and package smoke check.
All jobs use Node 20 (except verify which tests both 20 and 22) and rely on npm ci for reproducible installs.
The release gate job is a separate gate that must pass for a release to proceed, but actual publishing is not handled here.
